articles.title: Karnataka Weather Update: Bengaluru Rain Alert, Heatwave Warning in North Karnataka - Saptashwa TV
articles.full_text:
This will be another rain spell in Bengaluru today, which was confirmed by the India Meteorological Department (IMD) due to its new weather alert for the state of Karnataka.
In the next three days, most of the northern districts are expected to see harsh heatwave weather. Bengaluru and its surrounding districts are forecast to receive moderate rainfall along with strong winds and thunderstorms, the weather department said. The changing weather pattern offered some relief to people who had been suffering from the heat in the town, the state capital, for the last few days.
The IMD had projected rain in different areas of South Interior Karnataka with wind speeds of around 30–40 kmph. These regions are the districts of Bengaluru Urban, Bengaluru Rural, Chamarajanagar, Chikkaballapur, Chikkamagaluru, Chitradurga, Davanagere, Hassan, Kodagu, Kolar, Mandya, Mysuru, Ramanagara, Shivamogga, Tumakuru, Ballari, and Vijayanagara.
At the same time, coastal Karnataka like Udupi and Dakshina Kannada would also receive some moderate rainfall and combined would also get the thunderstorms. But weather officials at the same time have warned that some coastal areas may be subject to a heatwave, a combination of intense humidity, heat and rain.
In the northern part of Karnataka there is high heat. The weather department has said maximum temperatures of approximately 42 degrees Celsius will be recorded for areas like Kalaburagi, Raichur, Yadgir, Dharwad, Haveri and Gadag today. Officials say the same weather may be more protracted for the next three days.
Except for isolated rain in a few northern districts, the state is generally forecast to face dry weather. High temperatures and intermittent rain have brought a shaky atmosphere around the state. The rain alerts are following sudden evening showers with strong winds in many areas of Bengaluru several days earlier.
There have been local reports of traffic jams, waterlogging by bad circulation in flat areas and some localities’ short-term difficulties because of rain in earlier inclement weather. Health care providers in hot zones have warned people in recent heatwaves to stay hydrated, not to travel during the day and watch out for heat illnesses.
Elsewhere, residents in rain-prone areas can also be put on guard during thunderstorms and fierce winds. IMD surveillance over meteorological aspects is constantly working under these conditions, and they will be doing further to elaborate on the change of rainfall and temperature across Karnataka.
Residents should refer to official weather bulletins and prepare and protect themselves in their own personal plans to manage the risk of heatwave or thunderstorm if these are recommended.

articles.title: Rain alert for Bengaluru, heat wave in North Karnataka: IMD warns of mixed weather across state - NewsFirst Prime
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bagalkot OR "Ballari (Bellary)" OR "Belagavi (Belgaum)" OR "Bengaluru (Bangalore) Rural" OR "Bengaluru (Bangalore) Urban" OR Bidar OR Chamarajanagar OR Chikballapur OR "Chikkamagaluru (Chikmagalur)" OR Chitradurga OR "Dakshina Kannada" OR Davangere OR Dharwad OR Gadag OR Hassan OR Haveri OR "Kalaburagi (Gulbarga)" OR Kodagu OR Kolar OR Koppal OR Mandya OR "Mysuru (Mysore)" OR Raichur OR Ramanagara OR "Shivamogga (Shimoga)" OR "Tumakuru (Tumkur)" OR Udupi OR "Uttara Kannada (Karwar)" OR "Vijayapura (Bijapur)" OR Yadgir) Karnataka
articles.full_text:
Thunderstorms, strong winds and high temperatures expected across Karnataka; coastal and northern districts to see contrasting conditions over next three days.
Karnataka is set to witness a mix of extreme weather conditions, with rain in some regions and intense heat in others, according to the India Meteorological Department.
In Bengaluru, rain activity is expected to continue today, with the city already experiencing fresh spells. The weather department has issued a rain alert for the capital, warning of more showers accompanied by gusty winds.
At the same time, coastal districts are likely to see an unusual combination of heat and rain. Areas including Udupi and Dakshina Kannada are expected to receive light to moderate rainfall along with thunder. However, heat wave conditions have also been forecast in parts of the coast, making the weather pattern unpredictable.
In North Karnataka, the situation remains severe due to rising temperatures. Districts such as Kalaburagi and Raichur are likely to record maximum temperatures between 42°C and 42°C today. The department has cautioned that similar conditions may continue for the next three days. Heat wave alerts have been issued in several areas.
Apart from this, moderate rainfall accompanied by winds moving at 30-40 kmph is expected in several northern districts including Kalaburagi, Raichur, Yadgir, Dharwad, Haveri and Gadag.
Meanwhile, dry weather is likely to prevail in some other parts of the state.
In South Interior Karnataka, multiple districts are set to receive rainfall with thunder and gusty winds. These include Ballari, Bengaluru Rural, Bengaluru Urban, Chamarajanagar, Chikkaballapur, Chikkamagaluru, Chitradurga, Davanagere, Hassan, Kodagu, Kolar, Mandya, Mysuru, Ramanagara, Shivamogga, Tumakuru and Vijayanagara. Winds in these regions are expected to reach speeds of 30-40 kmph during rainfall.
The weather department has advised people to stay cautious as the state continues to experience varied and changing weather conditions over the coming days.
